text: Who doesn't like elegant? That feeling when your solution is -just right. Well, I do. I've worked several years teaching university courses, where each person requires a different approach, while keeping the institution's greater goals in mind. In my view, coding is a lot like that.

After more than a decade using paid software for numerical simulations, I started experimenting with open source software, mainly Python and Linux. I'm currently collaborating with a colleague in an education project based on Python, where teachers can code any number of problems, and students can randomly select them to solve. As I said, I'm starting, but I really want to learn and be useful to the community.
Thank you for your time. I look forward to learning more about the position and your organization's vision for this role.
